signals.idea: Lift the lightbox handler out of PreviewPanel and into VideoEditorProvider so it works regardless of which layout branch (condensed vs standard) is active. Currently PreviewPanel registers a lightbox handler via registerLightboxHandler in a useEffect, and we just added a CondensedLightboxProvider workaround in VideoEditorShell.tsx that duplicates the same logic. The fix: (1) Move the lightbox state (lightboxAssetKey, lightboxQuery, MediaLightbox rendering) from PreviewPanel.tsx into VideoEditorProvider.tsx where registerLightboxHandler already lives. Instead of a ref-based callback pattern, just expose setLightboxAssetKey directly through the ops context. (2) Remove the registerLightboxHandler ref pattern entirely from VideoEditorProvider.tsx — it's no longer needed since the provider owns the state directly. (3) Remove the lightbox state, handler registration useEffect, and MediaLightbox rendering from PreviewPanel.tsx. (4) Remove CondensedLightboxProvider from VideoEditorShell.tsx. (5) Update the onDoubleClickAsset in VideoEditorProvider.tsx to call setLightboxAssetKey directly instead of going through lightboxHandlerRef. (6) Keep the OverlayEditor's onDoubleClickAsset in PreviewPanel working — it should call the same context method. Key files: VideoEditorProvider.tsx, PreviewPanel.tsx, VideoEditorShell.tsx, useTimelineState.types.ts.

unresolved_flags.concern: Video editor lightbox tests: moving `useQuery` into `VideoEditorProvider` will break the existing provider unit test unless the plan also updates the test harness to supply React Query context.

registerLightboxHandler and lightboxHandlerRef are fully removed from codebase

No duplicate lightbox rendering — only one MediaLightbox instance exists in the tree
PreviewPanel.tsx shrinks by ~30 lines from removing lightbox code

unresolved_flags.concern: Video editor lightbox tests: moving `useQuery` into `VideoEditorProvider` will break the existing provider unit test unless the plan also updates the test harness to supply React Query context.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx:1` renders `<VideoEditorProvider>` directly with `@testing-library/react` and no `QueryClientProvider`, while the plan adds `useQuery` inside `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx:1`. In this repo, tests that exercise React Query hooks explicitly wrap with `QueryClientProvider`, e.g. `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.test.tsx:3`. Step 6 only mentions `tsc --noEmit` and a manual smoke test, so it does not cover or repair this existing test surface.
